questions.text: What is the correct mathematical recurrence relation to compute the intermediate error vector (delta) of a hidden layer l from the error vector of the subsequent layer l+1?
questions.explanation: Applying the multivariate chain rule shows that the error at layer l is the product of the transposed weight matrix of the next layer W^(l+1).T and the next error vector delta^(l+1), multiplied element-wise by the activation derivative of the current pre-activation vector z^(l).

questions.text: For a weight matrix W^(l) of shape (d_l, d_{l-1}), an error vector delta^(l) of shape (d_l, 1), and a preceding activation vector a^(l-1) of shape (d_{l-1}, 1), what is the correct formula and dimension for the gradient of the loss with respect to W^(l)?
questions.explanation: The gradient of the loss with respect to a weight parameter is the outer product of the error vector (delta) of the target layer and the transposed activation vector of the input layer. The dimensions are (d_l, 1) @ (1, d_{l-1}), yielding the shape (d_l, d_{l-1}) which matches W^(l).
